message:
fix(btrfs): correct RAID5/6 parity rotation for data stripe mapping

Rewrite resolve_raid56_stripe() to correctly handle parity position
wrap-around in RAID5/6 layouts. The previous arithmetic (incrementing
past parity_pos and taking modulo) failed when Q wrapped to position 0
in RAID6 configurations.

New approach:
- Build an explicit parity position set per row (P and optional Q)
- Walk device positions sequentially, skipping parity slots, to find
  the actual device index for a given data stripe
- Add documentation explaining the parity rotation scheme
- Remove unused `label` parameter from require_nonzero_stripe_len()

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
